UserProfile.title: UserProfile
UserProfile.description: Represents an administrator or staff member who uses the ServiceFlow Hub application. This entity stores application-specific user data, while authentication and security mechanisms like Google Authenticator are handled by an external system.

id.description: Unique identifier for the UserProfile entity.

firstName.description: The first name of the application user.

lastName.description: The last name of the application user.

email.description: The email address of the application user, used for internal communication and display.

phoneNumber.description: The contact phone number for the application user.

role.description: The role of the user within the application (e.g., 'Admin', 'Staff'), defining their permissions and access levels.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the user profile was last updated.

Client.title: Client
Client.description: Represents a customer for whom services are booked in the ServiceFlow Hub.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Client entity.

name.description: The full name of the client.

email.description: The primary contact email address for the client.

phoneNumber.description: The primary contact phone number for the client.

address.description: The physical address of the client.

notes.description: General notes or specific preferences about the client, which might influence future bookings.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the client record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the client record was last updated.

WorkType.title: WorkType
WorkType.description: Defines the different types of services offered by the business.

id.description: Unique identifier for the WorkType entity.

name.description: The name of the service (e.g., 'Web Design', 'Graphic Design', 'Consultation').

description.description: A detailed description of the service provided under this work type.

estimatedDurationHours.description: The estimated duration of the service in hours.

defaultPrice.description: A suggested default price for this service type, used as a baseline for new bookings.

isActive.description: Indicates if this work type is currently offered and available for booking.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the work type was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the work type was last updated.

Booking.title: Booking
Booking.description: Represents a scheduled appointment or service delivery for a client, including all financial and descriptive details.

id.description: Unique identifier for the Booking entity.

clientId.description: Reference to the Client entity for whom this booking is made. (Relationship: Client 1:N Booking)

workTypeId.description: Reference to the WorkType entity, specifying the service booked for this appointment. (Relationship: WorkType 1:N Booking)

bookingDate.description: The scheduled date of the booking.

bookingTime.description: The scheduled time of the booking.

advanceAmount.description: The amount paid in advance by the client for the booking.

totalAmount.description: The total agreed amount for the work or service for this booking.

expenseAmount.description: Any additional expenses incurred by the business for this specific booking.

expenseNote.description: A brief note or description explaining the expenses incurred for this booking.

balanceAmount.description: The amount remaining to be paid by the client for this booking (Total Amount - Advance Amount).

notes.description: Any additional notes or specific instructions related to this booking provided by the client or internal staff.

completionDetails.description: Detailed description of the work completed and any relevant outcomes for this booking, as per the client history requirement.

createdAt.description: Timestamp when the booking record was created.

updatedAt.description: Timestamp when the booking record was last updated.

definition.description: Stores application-specific profiles for staff and administrators. The '{userId}' wildcard MUST match 'request.auth.uid'. This path is for a user to manage their own profile. Authorization is path-based and ensures private data access. For broader role-based access checks, dedicated role collections are used.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the UserProfile (and Firebase Auth UID) that corresponds to the authenticated user.

definition.description: A dedicated collection for fast, permission-based checks. The existence of a document at `/adminRoles/{request.auth.uid}` indicates the authenticated user is an administrator. This avoids expensive 'get()' calls in security rules. The 'userId' MUST match 'request.auth.uid' and corresponds to the UserProfile.id.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the UserProfile (and Firebase Auth UID) that has the 'Admin' role.

definition.description: A dedicated collection for fast, permission-based checks. The existence of a document at `/staffRoles/{request.auth.uid}` indicates the authenticated user is a staff member. This avoids expensive 'get()' calls in security rules. The 'userId' MUST match 'request.auth.uid' and corresponds to the UserProfile.id.

params.name: userId
params.description: The unique identifier of the UserProfile (and Firebase Auth UID) that has the 'Staff' role.

definition.description: Stores information for all clients. Access is granted to UserProfiles with 'Admin' or 'Staff' roles. This collection has a homogeneous security posture, meaning all clients are accessible under the same rules by authorized personnel.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Client.

definition.description: Defines the various types of services offered. Access is granted to UserProfiles with 'Admin' or 'Staff' roles. This collection has a homogeneous security posture, meaning all work types are accessible under the same rules by authorized personnel.

params.name: workTypeId
params.description: The unique identifier for the WorkType.

definition.description: Stores specific booking details, nested under the respective Client. Each Booking document MUST contain a 'clientId' field that matches the '{clientId}' in its path. This structure enables efficient retrieval of a client's booking history and supports collectionGroup queries for global dashboards/reports. Includes denormalized 'clientId' for authorization independence.

params.name: clientId
params.description: The unique identifier of the Client to whom this booking belongs.

params.name: bookingId
params.description: The unique identifier for the Booking.

firestore.reasoning:
This Firestore structure is designed for a multi-user application (ServiceFlow Hub) with clear role-based access control, adhering to principles of Authorization Independence, Structural Segregation, and Access Modeling. 

**Authorization Independence:**
1.  **DBAC with Dedicated Role Collections:** To avoid expensive and non-atomic `get()` operations in security rules, user roles (`Admin`, `Staff`) are not solely determined by the `role` field in `/userProfiles/{userId}`. Instead, dedicated, 'empty' collections (`/adminRoles/{userId}` and `/staffRoles/{userId}`) are used. The *existence* of a document at `/adminRoles/$(request.auth.uid)` or `/staffRoles/$(request.auth.uid)` directly grants administrative or staff permissions respectively. This allows security rules to perform a fast `exists()` check, ensuring atomic authorization without relying on parent document data for every access decision. A backend function (e.g., Cloud Function) would be responsible for synchronizing these role collections with the `UserProfile.role` field whenever a user profile is created or updated.
2.  **Denormalized Subcollection Identifiers:** For subcollections like `/clients/{clientId}/bookings/{bookingId}`, the child document (`Booking`) includes the parent's identifier (`clientId`) as a field. This denormalization means that security rules for a `Booking` document can verify `request.resource.data.clientId == clientId` (where `clientId` is the path parameter) directly from the document itself, without needing to `get()` the parent `Client` document. This allows independent and atomic access checks for each booking.

**QAPs (Query-Able Permissions):**
1.  **User Profiles:** `/userProfiles/{userId}` allows users to access their own profile (`request.auth.uid == userId`) securely.
2.  **Admin/Staff Roles:** `/adminRoles/{userId}` and `/staffRoles/{userId}` are used for direct `exists()` checks in security rules to determine a user's role, enabling global `list` operations for authorized personnel across other data types (e.g., all `Clients`, all `WorkTypes`, all `Bookings`).
3.  **Client and WorkType Management:** `/clients/{clientId}` and `/workTypes/{workTypeId}` are designed as top-level collections, ensuring a homogeneous security posture. All documents within these collections require the same level of access (e.g., any staff or admin can view/edit). This allows simple `list` queries for all clients or work types, with rules checking the requesting user's role using the dedicated role collections.
4.  **Booking Management:** Bookings are stored hierarchically under clients at `/clients/{clientId}/bookings/{bookingId}`. This structure is ideal for the 'Client History View' as it allows efficient querying of all bookings for a specific client. For global views such as 'Upcoming Appointments Dashboard' and 'Financial Reporting Suite', `collectionGroup` queries on the `bookings` collection will be employed. Security rules for these `collectionGroup` queries will operate on the `Booking` document's denormalized `clientId` field and the requesting user's role, ensuring that `list` operations remain secure and efficient, and rules do not act as filters.

**Google Authenticator Note:** The request to 'add Google Authenticator' is an authentication mechanism and, as stated in the `UserProfile` schema, is handled by an external system (e.g., Firebase Authentication). This does not require any specific changes to the Firestore data structure itself, as Firestore stores user *data*, not authentication secrets or MFA states.
